projectDescription: An open-source framework that exposes real-world hardware capabilities to AI agents through standardized REST APIs, pluggable adapters, and a cloud relay registry.
keyFeatures: Standardized hardware capability API with dot-notation naming (e.g., camera.capture, radio.scan)
Pluggable adapter system for connecting any physical hardware device
Cloud relay registry for global hardware discovery and agent routing
Session-based exclusive hardware access with policy enforcement
Built-in MCP server for native LLM agent integration
CLI tooling with interactive TUI for node setup and management
WebSocket fast-path relay with HTTP long-poll fallback
Docker-first deployment with device passthrough support
Adapter scaffolding and conformance testing tools
publicApiSurface: GET /v1/capabilities — Discover available hardware devices and capabilities
POST /v1/provider/nodes/register — Register or heartbeat a node
OahlClient — JavaScript SDK for direct local node interaction
CloudClient — JavaScript SDK for cloud relay interaction
oahl init — Interactive node setup wizard
oahl start — Start local OAHL node daemon
oahl install <adapter> — Install a hardware adapter plugin
oahl create-adapter <name> — Scaffold a new adapter package
oahl scan-ports — Detect connected USB/serial devices
oahl mcp — Start MCP server for agent integration
oahl tui — Terminal UI for node configuration
oahl conformance — Run adapter conformance checks
Adapter interface: initialize(), healthCheck(), getDevices(), getCapabilities(), execute()
